Functions for initializing and controlling the PMIC, including power management and battery monitoring.

# Purpose
This C header file provides function declarations for managing a Power Management Integrated Circuit (PMIC) driver. It includes functions to initialize the PMIC ([`pmic_init`](<#pmic_init>)), retrieve the last reset reason ([`pmic_get_last_reset_reason`](<#pmic_get_last_reset_reason>)), and control power states such as powering off the board ([`pmic_power_off`](<#pmic_power_off>)). The file also includes functions to manage battery monitoring ([`pmic_enable_battery_measure`](<#pmic_enable_battery_measure>), [`pmic_disable_battery_measure`](<#pmic_disable_battery_measure>)) and charging states ([`pmic_set_charger_state`](<#pmic_set_charger_state>), [`pmic_is_charging`](<#pmic_is_charging>), [`pmic_is_usb_connected`](<#pmic_is_usb_connected>)). Additionally, it provides a function to read chip information ([`pmic_read_chip_info`](<#pmic_read_chip_info>)) and to get a voltage reading ([`pmic_get_vsys`](<#pmic_get_vsys>)). The file contains a note indicating that some functions related to power rail management ([`set_ldo3_power_state`](<#set_ldo3_power_state>), [`set_4V5_power_state`](<#set_4V5_power_state>), [`set_6V6_power_state`](<#set_6V6_power_state>)) are unrelated to the PMIC and should be moved to appropriate drivers in the future.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`
- `stdint.h`
